items.title: Simple, Smart & Actually Helpful for Beginners
items.contents:
Ive been using Groww for a while now, and honestly, its one of the easiest investing apps Ive come across. The interface is super clean and not confusing at all, which really helped me when I was just starting out with mutual funds and stocks.
What I like most is how straightforward everything feels — from KYC to making your first investment, the whole process is smooth and doesnt feel overwhelming. The app also provides basic explanations and insights, which are useful if you’re still learning the ropes.
That said, theres still room for improvement. Sometimes the app can lag a bit during peak market hours, and I wish they had more advanced tools for experienced investors. But for beginners and even intermediate users, it does the job really well.
Overall, Groww is a solid choice if you want a no-nonsense, user-friendly platform to start your investment journey without getting lost in complicated features.

items.title: Fraud App
items.contents:
I had 15k added to my wallet. And I saved it there cause I was unable to set up autopay. And later next month, I withdrew 5000 from the wallet. It said it will take 3-5 working days. I noticed the wallet balance was 10k. I waited patiently for 7 working days. No money was sent to my account. I immediately contacted the customer care. THEWORST CUSTOMER SERVICE TEAM I EVER CAME ACROSS. I explained them everything in detail and they promised me they will find a solution to my problem. Next day I got a mail from the team that the 10k in my account was transferred to my account and that the transaction is complete. I was in complete shock. The 5k I withdrew, didn’t touch my account. It was a painful journey from there. After writing several emails, I lost my cool. These guys were so stubborn and claimed that the money is sent to my account. I cried, cussed and begged for help. Still the response was the same. I had to let go of 5000 INR. Guys, trust me, this app is fraud. And don’t trust the rating. It’s not true. If you are someone who value money, don’t use this app. It’s a scam.

Edit: I saw your response. And I am talking about the money I added it to the wallet. Why and where did my 5000 gone? Is it as per SEBI regulations that you deducted that from my account??

response.contents: Hi, As per the current guidelines set by SEBI the SIP payments will not go through via Groww wallet. You will get the option to pay the SIP only via net/banking/ UPI or mandate.Please share the details of the issue by raising a ticket at support@groww.in Also share the ticket id here or you can call us on 9108800604. We’ll make sure to look into it with the utmost priority.

items.title: I first started my stock investment and SIP in this app only from 2023
items.contents:
I began my stock market investment journey and SIPs with the Groww trading app in 2023, and it has been a great experience so far. As a beginner, I found the app extremely user-friendly and easy to understand. The clean interface, simple navigation, and clear explanations made investing feel less intimidating.

Starting SIPs and investing in stocks was smooth, and tracking my portfolio has been very convenient. Over time, I have been able to accumulate funds consistently, and I’m really happy to see that my investments are growing steadily. The app provides helpful insights and updates that keep me informed about my investments.

Overall, Groww is a reliable and beginner-friendly app for anyone who wants to start their investment journey with confidence. I’m satisfied with my experience and happy with the progress of my funds.

items.title: Broker glitch
items.contents:
The app is good and user friendly but it has a major issue of closing up positions in trading.
I do think know if this happens to other people but many times when i try to exit my position, it won’t exit.
I do everything right, my strategy, stop loss, target, but when i reach my target and try to manually exit the position, it won’t exit, and no this is not always the case as sometimes the entry and exit are instant but in a high volume stock where there is so many shares and noise all over, the position becomes glitchy 
And it didn’t bothered me that much until today, i tried to exit my position at a profit of Rs240, which is not a lot for many of you, but for me with a 3k total capital in my account balance, THIS HURT, as my 240 profit turned into 23. I had to cancel the order because i panicked as the taxes and brokerage would eat up 40-50 rupees, so yeah i made a loss after trying to exit at 240 profit 
Now tell me, if a person like me who is trying to learn trading with a limited capital gets a 240 profit on a 3k account but then the app decides to slap his face, how would you guys feel.

items.title: Needs improvement
items.contents: I saw this app on the phone of a colleague and decided to download it as the UI looked clean and the options & calculators looked helpful. However, I encountered the same problem as any other Indian app which being, they rush to collect user data and get people to sign up without giving necessary information in the beginning. Main issue being, I am an NRI living in India currently and apparently this app doesn’t support NRI investing at the moment. This pop up - not really a pop up, it’s a small foot note which could have been easily missed or overlooked - only came after the app collected pretty much all KYC info like my PAN, email, phone number, Aadhar and now I am stuck at the signature screen. The most frustrating part is, there is no option to cancel the registration and delete my personal data or go back to main screen. I would be thankful if the developers can come up with a solution for this.

items.title: Utter chaos and ultimately loss making app
items.contents:
When I signed up, until I uploaded all the documents they kept reading me about it and once I uploaded they didn’t bother to do any KYC. But the dashboard didn’t show anything pending on my side. When I decided to checkout the app with a small amount, they waited until I transferred the money and then told me my ePAN cannot be accepted for KYC and kept my order pending. Note the NAV was around INR 27. Despite explaining on 2 calls they wouldn’t respect IT department directive that ePAN is equally valid. So I told them to refund the money, they replied we have initiated the refund and it will come back to account in 3 working days. Simultaneously another of their executive kept emailing me several times for me to email him the PAN scan.  I didn’t bother to reply as my refund was in progress as per the Team Lead who spoke last. Separately 2 days later I found the PAN scan and uploaded it in the app. Now suddenly on Monday 3 days after my request for refund, I receive a note saying the money has been sent to the Mutual Fund and units will be allocated in 3 or 4 days.  Finally the next day I receive a note saying I have been allocated x units @ NAV INR 29. Not only I had lost INR 2 per unit because of Groww’s mistakes and delays but also the cancellation that was initiated did not happen or they plainly lied to a customer. 

If this is how investors have to suffer at the hands of new age Brokers like Groww, I would rather desist from investing through such cheap apps. 

It’s funny their slogan is “proudly built in India for India.”  They are apparently in business from 2016. Maybe that’s their level of professionalism. But they should drag the country’s reputation along with it. I humbly request them to change their slogan.

review.title: Broker glitch
review.contents:
The app is good and user friendly but it has a major issue of closing up positions in trading.
I do think know if this happens to other people but many times when i try to exit my position, it won’t exit.
I do everything right, my strategy, stop loss, target, but when i reach my target and try to manually exit the position, it won’t exit, and no this is not always the case as sometimes the entry and exit are instant but in a high volume stock where there is so many shares and noise all over, the position becomes glitchy 
And it didn’t bothered me that much until today, i tried to exit my position at a profit of Rs240, which is not a lot for many of you, but for me with a 3k total capital in my account balance, THIS HURT, as my 240 profit turned into 23. I had to cancel the order because i panicked as the taxes and brokerage would eat up 40-50 rupees, so yeah i made a loss after trying to exit at 240 profit 
Now tell me, if a person like me who is trying to learn trading with a limited capital gets a 240 profit on a 3k account but then the app decides to slap his face, how would you guys feel.

review.title: Needs improvement
review.contents: I saw this app on the phone of a colleague and decided to download it as the UI looked clean and the options & calculators looked helpful. However, I encountered the same problem as any other Indian app which being, they rush to collect user data and get people to sign up without giving necessary information in the beginning. Main issue being, I am an NRI living in India currently and apparently this app doesn’t support NRI investing at the moment. This pop up - not really a pop up, it’s a small foot note which could have been easily missed or overlooked - only came after the app collected pretty much all KYC info like my PAN, email, phone number, Aadhar and now I am stuck at the signature screen. The most frustrating part is, there is no option to cancel the registration and delete my personal data or go back to main screen. I would be thankful if the developers can come up with a solution for this.

review.title: Utter chaos and ultimately loss making app
review.contents:
When I signed up, until I uploaded all the documents they kept reading me about it and once I uploaded they didn’t bother to do any KYC. But the dashboard didn’t show anything pending on my side. When I decided to checkout the app with a small amount, they waited until I transferred the money and then told me my ePAN cannot be accepted for KYC and kept my order pending. Note the NAV was around INR 27. Despite explaining on 2 calls they wouldn’t respect IT department directive that ePAN is equally valid. So I told them to refund the money, they replied we have initiated the refund and it will come back to account in 3 working days. Simultaneously another of their executive kept emailing me several times for me to email him the PAN scan.  I didn’t bother to reply as my refund was in progress as per the Team Lead who spoke last. Separately 2 days later I found the PAN scan and uploaded it in the app. Now suddenly on Monday 3 days after my request for refund, I receive a note saying the money has been sent to the Mutual Fund and units will be allocated in 3 or 4 days.  Finally the next day I receive a note saying I have been allocated x units @ NAV INR 29. Not only I had lost INR 2 per unit because of Groww’s mistakes and delays but also the cancellation that was initiated did not happen or they plainly lied to a customer. 

If this is how investors have to suffer at the hands of new age Brokers like Groww, I would rather desist from investing through such cheap apps. 

It’s funny their slogan is “proudly built in India for India.”  They are apparently in business from 2016. Maybe that’s their level of professionalism. But they should drag the country’s reputation along with it. I humbly request them to change their slogan.

review.title: Fraud App
review.contents:
I had 15k added to my wallet. And I saved it there cause I was unable to set up autopay. And later next month, I withdrew 5000 from the wallet. It said it will take 3-5 working days. I noticed the wallet balance was 10k. I waited patiently for 7 working days. No money was sent to my account. I immediately contacted the customer care. THEWORST CUSTOMER SERVICE TEAM I EVER CAME ACROSS. I explained them everything in detail and they promised me they will find a solution to my problem. Next day I got a mail from the team that the 10k in my account was transferred to my account and that the transaction is complete. I was in complete shock. The 5k I withdrew, didn’t touch my account. It was a painful journey from there. After writing several emails, I lost my cool. These guys were so stubborn and claimed that the money is sent to my account. I cried, cussed and begged for help. Still the response was the same. I had to let go of 5000 INR. Guys, trust me, this app is fraud. And don’t trust the rating. It’s not true. If you are someone who value money, don’t use this app. It’s a scam.

Edit: I saw your response. And I am talking about the money I added it to the wallet. Why and where did my 5000 gone? Is it as per SEBI regulations that you deducted that from my account??

response.contents: Hi, As per the current guidelines set by SEBI the SIP payments will not go through via Groww wallet. You will get the option to pay the SIP only via net/banking/ UPI or mandate.Please share the details of the issue by raising a ticket at support@groww.in Also share the ticket id here or you can call us on 9108800604. We’ll make sure to look into it with the utmost priority.

Main issue being, I am an NRI living in India currently and apparently this app doesn’t support NRI investing at the moment. This pop up - not really a pop up, it’s a small foot note which could have been easily missed or overlooked - only came after the app collected pretty much all KYC info like my PAN, email, phone number, Aadhar and now I am stuck at the signature screen. The most frustrating part is, there is no option to cancel the registration and delete my personal data or go back to main screen. I would be thankful if the developers can come up with a solution for this.

review.title: Utter chaos and ultimately loss making app
review.contents:
When I signed up, until I uploaded all the documents they kept reading me about it and once I uploaded they didn’t bother to do any KYC. But the dashboard didn’t show anything pending on my side. When I decided to checkout the app with a small amount, they waited until I transferred the money and then told me my ePAN cannot be accepted for KYC and kept my order pending. Note the NAV was around INR 27. Despite explaining on 2 calls they wouldn’t respect IT department directive that ePAN is equally valid. So I told them to refund the money, they replied we have initiated the refund and it will come back to account in 3 working days. Simultaneously another of their executive kept emailing me several times for me to email him the PAN scan.  I didn’t bother to reply as my refund was in progress as per the Team Lead who spoke last. Separately 2 days later I found the PAN scan and uploaded it in the app. Now suddenly on Monday 3 days after my request for refund, I receive a note saying the money has been sent to the Mutual Fund and units will be allocated in 3 or 4 days.  Finally the next day I receive a note saying I have been allocated x units @ NAV INR 29. Not only I had lost INR 2 per unit because of Groww’s mistakes and delays but also the cancellation that was initiated did not happen or they plainly lied to a customer. 

If this is how investors have to suffer at the hands of new age Brokers like Groww, I would rather desist from investing through such cheap apps. 

It’s funny their slogan is “proudly built in India for India.”  They are apparently in business from 2016. Maybe that’s their level of professionalism. But they should drag the country’s reputation along with it. I humbly request them to change their slogan.
